title: Comforting Venezuelan Pak Choi Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Venezuelan stew made with pak choi and cooked in a copper mug, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This dish is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the copper mug over medium heat.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chopped pak choi and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the crushed tomatoes, corn, black beans, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
Let the stew cool completely before refrigerating or freezing.
Reheat the stew over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Serve the stew with crusty bread or over rice, if desired.
